name: Glute Compound Shred
description: Heavy compound glute workout from @whoisthismommy — 5 big lifts (Bulgarian split squat, RDL, hip thrust, back extension, sumo squat) followed by cable and machine accessory work. Designed to hammer glutes from every angle: unilateral, hinge, thrust, and isolation. Pairs well as a glute-focused leg day or as a standalone lower body session.

exercises.notes: Dumbbell in each hand or barbell. Control the descent, drive through front heel.
Barbell or dumbbell. Slow eccentric, feel the hamstrings stretch, squeeze glutes at top.
Barbell across hips. Pause and squeeze at the top for 1 second.
Glute bias — round upper back slightly and think about squeezing glutes to lift, not lower back.
Wide stance, toes out. Hold heavy dumbbell. Push knees out, sit deep.
Squeeze at the top, control on the way back. No momentum.
Push knees wide, squeeze outer glutes. Slow eccentric.
Squeeze inner thighs together. Control on the way out.

tips: From @whoisthismommy — designed as a complete glute session
The 5 compound lifts hit glutes from every angle: unilateral (BSS), hinge (RDL), thrust, extension, and wide squat
Back extension glute bias: think about lifting with your glutes, not arching your lower back
Accessories are burnout — lighter weight, higher reps, chase the pump
Can replace the PPL legs day in Functional Bulk for a glute-focused week
